title: helianthemum cinereum subsp. cinereum
intro: Helianthemum cinereum subsp. cinereum, also known as rockrose, is a low-growing, woody shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its pink or white flowers and silvery-green foliage. The medicinal properties of rockrose include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and wounds, due to its ability to promote healing, reduce pain, and boost the immune system. Rockrose can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in water or a solvent, or by mixing them with other herbs and oils.
